---
title: "Logistic Regression"
author: "Graham I. Gordon"
date: "2026-05-08"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
library(boot)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(caret)
library(BART)
library(randomForest)
library(pls)
library(pROC)
```


```{r}
#clickstream <- read.csv(here("Data", "RawData", "e-shop_clothing2008.csv"),
#                        header = TRUE,
#                        sep = ";")



clickstream$price_2 <- as.factor( ifelse(clickstream$price_2 == 2, 0, clickstream$price_2) )

clickstream <- clickstream %>% mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor)) 

clickstream$price <- log(clickstream$price)
clickstream$month <- factor( format(as.Date(clickstream$date), '%m' ) )

colnames(clickstream)
```

```{r}
train <- sample( 1:nrow(clickstream), size = .7*nrow(clickstream) )
train_df <- clickstream[train, ]
test_df <- clickstream[-train, ]
table(clickstream$price_2)
```


```{r}
logmod <- glm( price_2 ~ main_category + as.factor(color) + location, family = binomial, data = clickstream )
#cv.log <- cv.glm( clickstream, logmod, K = 6 )

logprob <- predict(logmod, test_df, type = "response")
logpred <- ifelse(logprob > 0.5, 1, 0)

confusionMatrix(factor(logpred),
                factor(test_df$price_2))
```

```{r}
summary(logmod)
```


```{r}
factor_cols <- c("main_category", "color", "location", 
                 "model_photography", "page", "month")

# Convert in train and test
train_df[, factor_cols] <- lapply(train_df[, factor_cols], factor)
test_df[, factor_cols]  <- lapply(test_df[, factor_cols], factor)

# Then run your existing code as-is
keep_cols <- c("main_category", "color", "location", "price", 
               "model_photography", "page", "month")

xtrain <- train_df[, keep_cols]
ytrain <- train_df[, "hi_clicks_avg"] %>% factor()
xtest  <- test_df[, keep_cols]
ytest  <- test_df[, "hi_clicks_avg"] %>% factor()

```


```{r}
barfit <- pbart(xtrain,as.numeric(as.character(ytrain)), x.test = xtest)
```

```{r}
# Get predicted probabilities for test set
bart_probs <- barfit$prob.test.mean

# Convert to binary predictions
bart_pred <- ifelse(bart_probs > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(factor(bart_pred), factor(ytest), positive = '1')


roc_bart <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(ytest)), bart_probs)
auc(roc_bart)
plot(roc_bart, main = "BART ROC Curve")
```

For median: Accuracy : 0.6257  
For average: Accuracy : 0.6973

```{r}
rf_fit <- randomForest(x = xtrain, y = factor(ytrain),
                       ntree = 500,
                       sampsize = c("0" = 1000, "1" = 1000),
                       importance = TRUE)

# Get predicted probabilities for class 1
rf_prob <- predict(rf_fit, xtest, type = "prob")[, 2]

# ROC curve
roc_rf <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(ytest)), rf_prob)

# Plot it
plot(roc_rf, main = paste("Random Forest ROC — AUC:", round(auc(roc_rf), 3)))

bart_pred <- ifelse(bart_probs > 0.5, 1, 0)

confusionMatrix(factor(bart_pred), factor(ytest))
```
Random forest Accuracy : 0.6497 

```{r}
library(xgboost)

xgb_fit <- xgboost(
  x = as.matrix(xtrain %>% mutate(across(where(is.factor), as.numeric))),
  y = factor(as.numeric(as.character(ytrain))),
  nrounds = 100,
  objective = "binary:logistic",
  scale_pos_weight = sum(ytrain == 0) / sum(ytrain == 1),
  learning_rate = 0.1
)
```

```{r}
# Get predicted probabilities
xgb_prob <- predict(xgb_fit, 
                    newdata = as.matrix(xtest %>% mutate(across(where(is.factor), as.numeric))))

# Convert to binary predictions
xgb_pred <- ifelse(xgb_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(factor(xgb_pred), factor(as.numeric(as.character(ytest))))

# AUC
roc_xgb <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(ytest)), xgb_prob)
auc(roc_xgb)
plot(roc_xgb, main = paste("XGBoost ROC — AUC:", round(auc(roc_xgb), 3)))
```




```{r}
# 1. Build xtrain/xtest AFTER all preprocessing
keep_cols <- c("main_category", "color", "location", "price", 
               "model_photography", "page", "month")

xtrain <- data.frame(lapply(train_df[, keep_cols], as.numeric))
xtest  <- data.frame(lapply(test_df[, keep_cols],  as.numeric))
ytrain <- factor(train_df$hi_clicks_avg)
ytest  <- factor(test_df$hi_clicks_avg)

# 2. Verify dimensions before fitting
dim(xtrain)   # e.g. 104248 x 7
dim(xtest)
levels(ytrain)  # "0" "1"

# 3. Fit
plsda.fit <- train(x          = xtrain,
                   y          = ytrain,
                   method     = "pls",
                   tuneLength = 5,
                   trControl  = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10),
                   preProcess = c("center", "scale"))

# 4. Predict and evaluate
pred_class <- predict(plsda.fit, newdata = xtest)
confusionMatrix(pred_class, ytest)
```
